what_it_does: This file exports a React component that renders a time-scrubber control. It maps a date range between a corpus start time and the current time onto a 0-to-1 slider, lets the user pick a historical timestamp, and treats the far-right position as a live present state (represented by a null `at` value). It also exports a small helper that formats ISO timestamps into a readable local date/time string.
observations: Imports `stamp` from `../lib/address` and spreads it onto the root `<div>` as a data attribute.
Renders `null` when `corpusStart` or `now` are missing, or when `now` is not after `start`.
Uses `Date.parse` for all date conversions and `toISOString` when setting a new timestamp.
Slider `step` is `0.001` and snapping to the live present occurs when the fraction is `>= 0.999`.
The label shows a formatted `at` timestamp or the literal text `now · live`.
A `now` button appears only when a past timestamp is selected and resets `at` to `null`.
Adds a `scrubber--past` class only when `at` is set.
Includes an `aria-label` on the range input and a `title` on the reset button.
standouts: The live-present-as-null convention and the 0.999 snap threshold.
Use of `stamp('ui://controls/scrubber')` to annotate the rendered control.
Fine-grained temporal slider backed only by start/end timestamps.
conventions: Functional React component with props destructured in the signature.
Early-return guard clauses for missing or invalid inputs.
Locale formatting delegated to `Date.prototype.toLocaleString` with `undefined` locale.
concerns: `Date.parse` behavior is implementation-sensitive for non-ISO strings; no explicit ISO validation is performed.
The 0.999 snap threshold is a magic number with no documented rationale.
If `at` lies outside `[corpusStart, now]`, the displayed label can represent a value outside the slider range even though the slider fraction is clamped.
No debouncing or throttling on the range input `onChange`.
`toISOString` produces UTC output while `toLocaleString` displays local time, which may confuse users crossing timezone boundaries.
notes: A small, focused temporal slider that treats the present as the default and historical selections as deviations.
questions: What does `stamp` do and what consumes the `ui://controls/scrubber` marker?
How are `corpusStart` and `now` produced and kept in sync with the actual data corpus?
Where is the `?at=` query parameter read and written relative to this component?
Why was `0.999` chosen as the snap threshold rather than `1` or a configurable value?
What should happen if `at` is before `corpusStart` or after `now`?
purpose_vs_actual: The comment describes a time scrubber that projects only data existing by the chosen timestamp (`ts ≤ at`) with a pure-read `?at=` parameter. The file itself only implements the slider control and timestamp formatting; the projection/filtering logic lives elsewhere, so the stated purpose is partially realized here.
intention_signals: The component appears designed to let users intuitively scrub backward through a corpus timeline while keeping the live present as the zero-effort default, and to mark the control with a stable UI identifier for instrumentation or addressing.
novelty: The combination of a normalized temporal slider with a near-present snap that collapses the selection back to a null/live state, plus the use of a `stamp` utility to attach a URI-like identifier to a DOM element.
contribution: It contributes a reusable, self-contained time-scrubber UI that converts a date range into a normalized slider, distinguishes live-present from historical-selection states, and exposes a reset action.
summary_for_embedding: A React slider component that selects a historical timestamp within a corpus date range, defaulting to a live present state and formatting the selected time for display.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a minimal UI for scrubbing backward through time to view the corpus as it existed at a chosen moment, while keeping the present as the default.

symbols.does: Formats an ISO timestamp into a short locale date/time string, falling back to the original string on error.
symbols.description: `fmt` is a small helper that takes an ISO string, parses it as a `Date`, and returns a localized representation using month, day, hour, and minute. If parsing fails, it returns the input unchanged.

symbols.does: Renders a slider control for choosing a timestamp between a corpus start and the current time, with the rightmost position representing the live present.
symbols.description: `Scrubber` is the exported React component. It guards against missing or invalid start/end dates, computes a normalized slider position, renders a range input and a reset-to-now button, and applies a `stamp` data attribute to the root element.

symbols.does: Converts a slider fraction back into an ISO timestamp, or clears the selection when the slider is near the live-present end.
symbols.description: `onChange` is an inner callback attached to the range input. It maps the 0-to-1 fraction to a date within the corpus range using linear interpolation; when the fraction is at least `0.999` it sets `at` to `null` to represent the live present.
